vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1908: event-driven agent state — SQLite runtime registry + git-permanent issue records in the infra repo (big-bang)

plan.title: feat(architecture): event-driven agent state — SQLite runtime registry + git-permanent issue records in the infra repo (big-bang)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Agent + per-issue pipeline state lives on the filesystem-as-registry: ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json files are simultaneously the data AND the only way to enumerate agents, forcing O(all-agents-ever) synchronous directory scans on 5s/60s hot paths. This stalls the dashboard event loop (24s /api/resources), triggers watchdog restarts and WebSocket reconnect storms (PAN-1711, PAN-1436, PAN-1846), and diverges across state.json / SQLite / tmux / review-status.json (PAN-1883, PAN-1888). There is no canonical infra repo and no machine-to-machine portability (PAN-541, PAN-111, PAN-1325).
narratives.Proposal: Move every datum into exactly one of three planes. RUNTIME (local SQLite ~/.panopticon/panopticon.db): a new authoritative `agents` table becomes the enumeration+status registry, updated event-driven via a write-through transactional projection — no directory scans, no patrol polling. PERMANENT (git, a declared infra repo): a per-issue vBRIEF record (continue + pipeline status + close-out + owner-URI lease) is autocommitted, making issues portable. LIVENESS (tmux): unchanged. Big-bang cutover (not phased dual-write): add the table, back the choke-points, emit lifecycle events, delete the patrol scans, declare the infra repo, write the permanent records, backfill, and remove confirmed-legacy fields. Reuse vBRIEF v0.6 structures throughout; extend the existing event store + reactive scheduler rather than inventing new machinery.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Conversations stay machine-local SQLite (conversations table); they are NOT made portable here (FR-8).
- Multi-node owner-URI reclaim-timeout tuning and heartbeat wiring are deferred; only the single-machine claim/refuse/clear slice ships (CP-3).
- channels_enabled and the Claude Code Channels delivery path are KEPT (live: written agents.ts:3727, read agents.ts:1522); their removal is a separate post-Channels-retirement effort.
- getAgentDir and the per-agent directory stay; sidecar artifacts (pty-token, transcripts, output.log, launcher.sh) are NOT moved out of the directory.
- The docker-stats 5s poll stays (container telemetry, not agent state).
- The ~90 §12c out-of-scope issues (event-store retention, log rotation, remote/fly, merge-train, lifecycle-classification) are untouched.
- §12b remainders are NOT delivered: specialist .session files + compact-offsets (PAN-541), the stop-hook review-status.json writer (PAN-1888), per-project infra setup + docs/prds (PAN-1325), and the other narrowed sub-deliverables.
- No work/implementation bead closes GitHub issues or clears pipeline/merge rows; §12 execution is the post-merge close-out ceremony, captured only as a runbook.
- No phased dual-write store: this is a single big-bang cutover.

autoDecisions.summary: CP-2: keep state.json written through cutover (not dropped) as the rollback source per §8a; final disposition deferred to the choke-point bead.
autoDecisions.rationale: Big-bang has no dual-write safety; keeping the old enumeration source until the new registry is proven is the defensible default.

autoDecisions.summary: CP-3: implement only the single-machine owner-URI slice (write own URI on claim, refuse live-other-owned, clear at close-out); defer multi-node reclaim/heartbeat.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD scopes tonight to single-machine correctness; multi-node tuning is a separable follow-up.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope stays one big-bang issue covering both runtime and permanent planes, per the PRD title and FR-1..FR-8.
autoDecisions.rationale: The runtime cutover cannot be phased; the PRD is the operator's own authored decision. Recorded as hazard H1 with the permanent plane as the carve point if review size forces a fast-follow.

items.title: Add the SQLite `agents` table + indexes + migration (authoritative runtime registry)

narrative.Action: Add the SQLite `agents` table with every column listed in PRD §5.1 plus idx_agents_status_role and idx_agents_issue indexes, and its forward migration in src/lib/database/schema.ts, so it becomes the authoritative runtime registry replacing state.json enumeration.

items.title: Given a fresh database, running migrations creates the agents table with every column listed in PRD §5.1 (id PRIMARY KEY through updated_at).
The migration creates indexes idx_agents_status_role on (status, role) and idx_agents_issue on (issue_id).
The schema preserves channels_enabled as an INTEGER column and contains no codexMode or preSpawnStash columns.
Re-running migrations on an existing database preserves existing rows and creates no duplicate table (idempotent).
Create src/lib/database/agents-db.ts data-access module + unit tests

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/database/agents-db.ts mirroring conversations-db.ts, exposing upsert, get-by-id, list-by-status-and-role, count-by-role, and delete-by-id functions over the agents table, covered by a vitest unit suite.

items.title: upsertAgent persists a row keyed by id and returns the stored shape (AC-1).
getAgent returns the row for a known id and returns null for an unknown id.
listAgentsByStatusRole returns only rows matching the given status and role via the indexed query.
A vitest unit suite exercises upsert/get/list/count and passes.
Back getAgentState/saveAgentState (sync+async) with SQLite, dual-writing state.json through cutover

narrative.Action: Rewrite getAgentState/getAgentStateSync and saveAgentState/saveAgentStateSync in src/lib/agents.ts to read from and write to agents-db, still writing state.json through cutover (D2/CP-2) and never re-introducing the non-atomic writeFileSync registry write at agents.ts:1080.

items.title: getAgentStateSync returns an agent's state by reading the agents table, not by reading state.json.
saveAgentStateSync persists the row to the agents table transactionally and also writes state.json (kept as the rollback source per §8a).
Given an agent saved via saveAgentState, getAgentState returns the same fields back.
Running the existing agents and concurrency suites produces no new failures.
Back listRunningAgentsSync + countRunningAgents with indexed agents-table queries

narrative.Action: Rewrite listRunningAgentsSync (agents.ts) and countRunningAgents (concurrency.ts) to answer from indexed agents-table queries instead of scanning ~/.panopticon/agents directories, ending the O(all-agents-ever) enumeration.

items.title: countRunningAgents returns per-role counts from a single GROUP BY query against the agents table.
Grepping the rewritten listRunningAgentsSync shows it returns rows from an indexed SELECT and contains no readdir.
Given 3 running and 2 stopped agents in the table, listRunningAgentsSync returns exactly the 3 running rows.
Define agent-lifecycle event payload schemas + reducers (the event protocol consumers depend on)

narrative.Action: Add typed payload schemas for agent.started (full initial row), agent.status_changed (agentId + changed fields), agent.stopped, and agent.heartbeat_dead to packages/contracts/src/events.ts, plus the reducers projecting them onto the agents read-model, matching the live taxonomy without inventing new event names.

items.title: packages/contracts/src/events.ts contains typed payload schemas for agent.started, agent.status_changed, agent.stopped, and agent.heartbeat_dead.
The agent.status_changed payload contains only the agentId plus changed fields, and the reducer applies it by merging only present fields.
The reducer preserves absent columns so a partial event never nulls a field it omits.
Replaying the same lifecycle event twice produces an identical projected row (idempotent).
Implement the write-through transactional projection (row upsert + event append in one tx)

narrative.Action: Implement the write-through transactional boundary so every lifecycle transition upserts the agents row AND appends its event inside one SQLite transaction, with idempotent partial-field merge, replacing append-then-async-project (which a crash could leave stale).

items.title: Each lifecycle transition commits the agents-row upsert and the event append inside one SQLite transaction (AC-11).
Given a simulated crash between the row upsert and the event append, a test shows the row and event log cannot disagree.
Replaying any lifecycle event preserves the resulting agents row unchanged (idempotent).
A partial event applied to an existing row preserves the columns it does not mention.
Route the agent lifecycle emit sites through the transactional projection boundary

narrative.Action: Route the existing agent lifecycle emit sites (spawn/start/stop/status-change/fail in routes/agents.ts and lib/agents.ts) through the transactional projection boundary so they emit the new payloads and update the agents row atomically.

items.title: Spawning an agent emits agent.started carrying the full initial row and persists that row.
Stopping an agent emits agent.stopped and updates the row status to stopped.
A heartbeat-death detection emits agent.heartbeat_dead for the dead agent.
Replace autoResume + recoverOrphaned patrol scans with agent.stopped/heartbeat_dead handlers

narrative.Action: Replace the autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents and recoverOrphanedAgents directory-scan patrol steps in deacon.ts with handlers subscribed to agent.stopped and agent.heartbeat_dead events, so liveness recovery reacts to events rather than scanning agent directories.

items.title: When agent.stopped fires for a work agent with pending feedback, the deacon resumes it without any directory scan.
Receiving agent.heartbeat_dead marks the agent stopped and triggers recovery without scanning agent directories.
Grepping deacon.ts shows the autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents and recoverOrphanedAgents scan bodies are deleted from the patrol.
Replace checkOrphaned/MissingReviewStatuses scans with review/work event handlers

narrative.Action: Replace the checkOrphanedReviewStatuses and checkMissingReviewStatuses patrol scans in deacon.ts with handlers reacting to review.status_changed, review.coordinator.died, and work.completed events, so orphan/missing review rows are handled reactively.

items.title: When review.coordinator.died fires, the deacon re-dispatches review for that issue without re-scanning the review-status DB.
When work.completed fires for a work agent, a missing review-status row is created reactively.
Grepping deacon.ts shows the checkOrphanedReviewStatuses and checkMissingReviewStatuses scan bodies are deleted.
Replace orphan-proposed / closed-issue / idle-stack / session-cleanup scans with event handlers

narrative.Action: Replace the orphan-proposed-spec, closed-issue, idle-stack, and orphaned planning/review session patrol scans with event handlers keyed on spec-finalized/plan-status, issue.statusChanged(closed), and agent.stopped/started events.

items.title: When a spec reaches status proposed, the work agent spawns from that event rather than from a spec-directory scan.
When issue.statusChanged(closed) fires, the deacon stops that issue's agent without a reconcile scan.
Grepping deacon.ts and orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts shows the converted scan bodies are deleted.
Reduce the 60s deacon patrol to a thin dropped-event reconciler

narrative.Action: Reduce the 60s deacon patrol to a thin reconciler that only catches dropped events, keeping the dropped-event fallback while the former per-step scans now run as event handlers (per §6.1 — do not delete the patrol entirely).

items.title: The 60s patrol contains only a thin reconciler pass and no per-agent directory enumeration.
Given a dropped lifecycle event, the safety-net patrol still recovers the affected agent on its next pass.
The deacon.log no longer emits a per-agent skip line every 60s.
Rewrite GET /api/resources to query the agents table + push resources.updated

narrative.Action: Rewrite GET /api/resources in routes/resources.ts to answer from the agents table and push a resources.updated event to WebSocket subscribers instead of performing the per-request state.json readdir sweep.

items.title: GET /api/resources returns the active-agent set from a single SELECT against the agents table.
Grepping routes/resources.ts shows no readdir of the agents directory remains.
A resource change emits a resources.updated event to subscribers.
Drive read-model reconcile + running-agents cache from lifecycle events

narrative.Action: Drive the dashboard read-model agent reconcile and the running-agents cache (running-agents-cache.ts) from agent.started/agent.stopped events instead of periodic tmux/state.json diffing, reconciling against tmux only once at bootstrap.

items.title: An agent.started event updates the read-model running-agent set and agent.stopped removes it, without a periodic tmux sweep.
running-agents-cache.ts is populated from lifecycle events and contains no directory scan.
Given the read-model bootstrap, agent liveness reconciles against tmux once at startup and thereafter updates on events.
Add projects.yaml pan_records declaration + infra-repo resolver

narrative.Action: Add the pan_records (repo + path) declaration to projects.yaml and a resolver mapping a project to its infra-repo checkout, defaulting monorepos to '.' and selecting a named entry from repos[] for polyrepos (§4.2).

items.title: A monorepo project with no pan_records defaults to the project repo itself ('.').
A polyrepo project's pan_records.repo resolves to the matching entry in its repos[] list.
The resolver returns the infra-repo checkout path and the .pan subdir for a given project.
Build the per-issue permanent-record writer + queueAutoCommit repo-target selection

narrative.Action: Build the per-issue permanent-record writer (continue + pipeline + closeOut + owner shape from §4.1) and extend queueAutoCommit in src/lib/pan-dir/auto-commit.ts to support repo-target selection so .pan records commit to the declared infra repo rather than unconditionally the project repo.

items.title: The writer produces a single per-issue record file shaped per §4.1 (durable continue subset + pipeline + closeOut + owner).
queueAutoCommit accepts a repo target and commits .pan records to the declared infra repo.
The record preserves the durable continue subset and contains none of the runtime-only fields (resumePoint, sessionHistory, agentModel, beadsMapping).
Committing a record to the infra repo preserves the existing main-only, debounced, rebase-after-commit behavior.
Route durable review_status verdicts into the per-issue record's pipeline block

narrative.Action: Route the 23 durable review_status verdict/override columns (§5.3) into the per-issue record's pipeline block on change, while the 16 ephemeral columns stay in the SQLite review_status table.

items.title: When a durable review_status column changes, the per-issue record's pipeline block records the new verdict and commits to the infra repo.
The pipeline block contains the five notes columns and the three deacon_ignored columns (durable tier).
The SQLite review_status table preserves the 16 ephemeral columns and the git record contains none of them.
Aggregate per-issue close-out usage.byStage + merges[] + ranOn

narrative.Action: Aggregate per-issue cost_events into closeOut.usage.byStage (GROUP BY session_type, provider, model filtered by issue_id), produce forge-agnostic merges[] URLs from merge_set_repos, bucket unknown session_type under stage 'other', and stamp ranOn.

items.title: Close-out produces usage.byStage raw token counts keyed by provider/model and bucketed by pipeline stage for the issue.
Close-out routes cost_events rows with null/unknown session_type into stage 'other' and drops none.
Close-out produces a forge-agnostic merges[] array of URL strings from merge_set_repos rows.
Given the stored usage counts, the close-out dollar cost recomputes deterministically (not stored as source of truth).
Implement the minimal single-machine owner-URI lease (claim / refuse / clear)

narrative.Action: Implement the minimal owner-URI lease (CP-3): write pan://host[:port] to the per-issue record on claim, refuse to drive an issue owned by a live other URI, and clear the owner at close-out — single-machine correctness only.

items.title: Claiming an issue persists this node's owner URI (pan://host[:port]) into the per-issue record.
A node refuses to drive an issue whose owner is a live, different URI (AC-12).
Close-out deletes the owner value from the per-issue record.
Add the no-loss audit test (every AgentState field + review_status column maps to one home)

narrative.Action: Add a blocking test that maps every AgentState field and every review_status column to exactly one home (agents-column, pipeline-block route, ephemeral-SQLite, or explicit DELETE list) and fails when any field is unclassified, so the big-bang cannot silently drop a field. Appendix A §1/§2 is the manifest.

items.title: The test fails when any of the 39 AgentState fields is unclassified across {agents-column, pipeline-route, ephemeral, delete-list}.
The test fails when any of the 39 review_status columns is unaccounted for in the durable/ephemeral/delete split.
Adding a new unclassified AgentState field fails the audit test (red).
Grep of the migration and backfill shows no read or write of the conversations table (FR-8).
Delete dead preSpawnStash* fields and collapse codexMode to the launcher default

narrative.Action: Delete the dead preSpawnStashRef/Message/BaselineHead fields and their deserialize/copy-on-respawn code, and collapse codexMode to the single launcher default 'work-tui' (drop 'exec'/'tui' from the persisted union), per the Appendix A verdicts.

items.title: The AgentState interface contains no preSpawnStashRef, preSpawnStashMessage, or preSpawnBaselineHead fields.
Grep finds no remaining references to the deleted preSpawn fields and typecheck passes.
codexMode no longer persists to the agents table and collapses to the launcher default 'work-tui'.
Remove interface-only review_status legacy fields and reconcile lifetimeAutoRequeueCount

narrative.Action: Remove the interface-only review_status legacy fields (queuePosition, lastReviewCommits) from the ReviewStatus interface and reconcile lifetimeAutoRequeueCount to the single backing lifetime_auto_requeue_count column, per Appendix A §2d.

items.title: The ReviewStatus interface contains no queuePosition or lastReviewCommits fields.
lifetimeAutoRequeueCount resolves to the single lifetime_auto_requeue_count column (no phantom interface field).
Typecheck passes and grep finds no references to the removed fields.
One-time versioned backfill of the agents table + pan admin rebuild-agents

narrative.Action: Add the one-time versioned backfill that enumerates ~/.panopticon/agents/*/state.json into the agents table (the only permitted directory enumeration), reconciles status against live tmux, is idempotent by id, and provides a pan admin rebuild-agents rebuild path; a test asserts no hot path reaches the enumeration.

items.title: Running the backfill persists one agents-table row per existing state.json, keyed by id.
Re-running the backfill preserves rows and creates no duplicates (idempotent by id).
The backfill marks an agent stopped when no live tmux session matches its id.
A test shows no dashboard/deacon hot path reaches the directory enumeration (AC-2).
Backfill in-flight per-issue permanent records into the infra repo

narrative.Action: Backfill the per-issue permanent records for in-flight issues into the infra repo from the current .pan/continues and review_status data, committing them via the new repo-target writer.

items.title: For each in-flight issue, the backfill produces a per-issue record combining .pan/continues and review_status data.
The backfill commits the per-issue records to the declared infra repo.
Re-running the records backfill preserves existing records and creates no duplicates.
Cutover safety net — pre-migrate DB snapshot + kill-switch fallback

narrative.Action: Provide the big-bang safety net (§8a): snapshot panopticon.db before migrating and a kill-switch flag that disables the event-driven deacon and falls back to no-resume mode without data loss.

items.title: The migration creates a file-copy snapshot of panopticon.db before altering the agents data.
Setting the kill-switch flag falls back to no-resume mode and disables the event-driven deacon.
Losing the SQLite DB preserves all permanent data (the git record + tmux rebuild the runtime plane).
Write docs/AGENT-STATE-PLANES.md (canonical three-plane description)

narrative.Action: Write the new docs/AGENT-STATE-PLANES.md describing the permanent/runtime/liveness three-plane model, the agents table, and the infra-repo (pan_records) configuration.

items.title: docs/AGENT-STATE-PLANES.md exists and contains a section for each of the three planes (permanent/runtime/liveness).
The doc contains the infra-repo (pan_records) configuration shape.
Revise CLAUDE.md + docs/VBRIEF.md for the three-plane model and per-issue record

narrative.Action: Revise CLAUDE.md and docs/VBRIEF.md so the four-artifact/agent-state sections describe the three-plane model and the per-issue record carrying pipeline + closeOut + owner blocks, with spec/continue now living in the infra repo.

items.title: CLAUDE.md contains the three-plane model in place of the directory-as-registry description.
docs/VBRIEF.md contains the pipeline/closeOut/owner blocks and the infra-repo location for spec/continue.
Revise roles/flywheel + ROLES/FLYWHEEL/MISSION-CONTROL/KANBAN docs to drop scan/review-status.json language

narrative.Action: Revise docs/ROLES.md, docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md, docs/MISSION-CONTROL.md, docs/KANBAN-MODEL.md, roles/flywheel.md, and docs/flywheel-brief.md to remove central review-status.json / directory-scan language and point at the agents table + git records.

items.title: Grepping the listed docs shows no remaining 'central review-status.json' or directory-scan language.
roles/flywheel.md contains the rule that pipeline truth lives in SQLite/git records, never review-status.json.
Mark STATE-STORAGE-AUDIT resolved + archive NewAgentApproach.md as superseded

narrative.Action: Mark docs/STATE-STORAGE-AUDIT.md resolved with a link to this PRD and add a superseded-by-PAN-1908 banner to NewAgentApproach.md (it is not a live source; this PRD is self-contained).

items.title: docs/STATE-STORAGE-AUDIT.md contains a resolved marker linking to PAN-1908.
NewAgentApproach.md contains a superseded-by-PAN-1908 banner.
Write the post-merge close-out runbook for the §12 superseded/narrowed issues (no execution at work time)

metadata.acJustification: Four ACs are needed: three runbook-content checks (the §12a close steps, the pipeline-row cleanup, the §12b relabels) plus the critical guard that no implementation bead executes gh issue close during the work phase.

narrative.Action: Write the post-merge close-out runbook listing the five §12a issues with exact gh issue close commands and review_status/merge-row cleanup, plus the twelve §12b relabel steps — for execution during the post-merge close-out ceremony, never during implementation (D5).

items.title: The runbook contains all five §12a issues with their exact gh issue close commands referencing this PRD/PR.
The runbook contains the review_status/merge-row cleanup steps for each §12a issue.
The runbook contains the twelve §12b relabel-to-remaining-scope steps and marks them do-not-close.
Grepping the branch diff shows no gh issue close invocation in any implementation bead.
